channel.title: Is this real life?
channel.description: Recent content on Is this real life?

item.title: 2D Seismic lagrangian model
item.description: About this project 2D Lagrangian model that aims to capture nonlinear soil-structure interactions given a seismic excitation. The structure was modeled by means of dashpots ands springs. The equations of movement were further determined using Lagrangian mechanics. Four problems are tackled, the first three are initial conditions problems (that try to emulate the seismic record maximum inputs), while the fourth problem describes the physical behavior of the structure given a real earthquake record

item.title: Mechanical, mineralogical, morphological and computational characterization of Ecuadorian Soil
item.description: About this project This is a first attempt to generate a nationwide data baseline of the mechanical, mineralogical and morphological characterization of Ecuadorian soil. With this aim, the analysis of unaltered soil samples corresponding to the capitals of twenty-three provinces and six other significant locations in Ecuador is performed as follows: First, the gradation and Atterberg limits allow us to obtain each sample’s classification according to the Unified Soil Classification System (USCS), which provides important information regarding soil’s mechanical behavior.

item.title: A one-to-one Concrete Sample Computational Avatar
item.description: About this project From a real concrete sample, we extract 3DXRCT images from which we isolate the aggregate pixels. First, by processing the CT images and then by transforming the aggregates into scalar mathematical functions called Level-Sets. Once we have reconstructed the concrete cylinder using the aggregate Level-Sets we are ready to make a uniaxial computational simulation and compare it with the real concrete cylinder test. These is how fairly well processed images look like, these is actually a concrete cylinder.

item.title: Image processing
item.description: About this project From a real granular sample we extract 3DXRCT images and then isolate the grains pixels. First, we process the CT images to further transform the grain pixels into scalar mathematical functions called Level-Sets. The samples depicted are Jaramijó Secondary Lahars (Ecuador soil) and the respective 3D pixel reconstruction of those slices. However, the main problem with these slices is that they are raw images, therefore they are really noisy which makes really difficult to know what is a grain or empty space.

item.title: Linear elasticity
item.description: About this project This is a simple, ‘from-scratch’ and (maybe) hardcoded FE script, nonetheless it gets the job done. This is one of the first scripts I did for the Theoretical and Computational Mechanics Group. There is not much to say, but that the code is thought for both planar stress or planar strain. The following example includes a one sided fixed plate pulled by a opposite side distributed load. The tackled problem is shown in the following figure.

item.title: Nonlinear double pendulum
item.description: About this project Simulation of a nonlinear and linear double pendulum. It is important to mention that the animation was made in Matlab. The solution of the equations that describe the movement of the double pendulum were coded from scratch making use of classic numerical methods. A lagragian physics framework was used in order to obtain the equations that describe the behaviour of the double pendulum. In the case of the nonlinear pendulum, the equations were first discretized by finite differences and then a multivariable Newton-Raphson (NR) solver was implemented each tilmestep in order to solve the nonlinear system of equations that arise from the FD scheme discretization.
